comparison_profile.objective: Compare horizontal fan-out overhead against the durable monolith.

workload_model.payload_shape: Same fixed-size benchmark payload as Labs 01 and 02.
workload_model.concurrency_model: Deterministic virtual users with one message every 1000 ms in the comparison run.

metrics_contract.error_rate: Dropped messages plus DB errors divided by total processed messages.
metrics_contract.db_latency: Optional, reported only when persistence is in the path.

traceability.contract: trace_id and message_id survive Redis publish and subscriber rebroadcast.
traceability.propagation: Client -> node -> Redis bus -> peer node -> client echo.

consistency_model.notes: Redis pub/sub favors fast fan-out over durable cross-node ordering guarantees.

failure_model.focus: Broker disconnects, duplicate fan-out, and subscriber lag.

routing_strategy.policy: Shared pub/sub bus with node-local websocket fan-out.
routing_strategy.objective: Decouple websocket ingress from cluster-wide fan-out.

observability.baseline: Prometheus metrics plus duplicate detection in benchmark summaries.
observability.logging: Message IDs identify pub/sub duplicates and echo completion.

cost_model.dominant_axis: Broker CPU, memory, and cross-node network traffic.
cost_model.notes: Horizontal fan-out shifts cost from one process to the messaging layer.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Fan-out scales better than a mutex-bound monolith, but consistency becomes eventual.
synthesis.learning_goal: Introduce the first distributed coordination trade-off.

comparison_standard.description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.
